_meta.purpose: Step-3 lookup for the phe-estimator rubric: convert a protein source class to a phenylalanine-per-gram-of-protein basis. Phe is a roughly constant fraction of protein (~45-55 mg/g) across most sources, which is why label protein is a usable starting point; the class variation is what label protein alone misses, and what this table restores.
_meta.provenance: Classes present in the seed test set are grounded in USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) nutrient 508 (Phenylalanine) / 203 (Protein) ratios computed from testset/food_reference.json. Classes not yet represented use documented typical amino-acid-composition values and are marked accordingly; they are refined as the living food list grows. This is a Layer-1 (cited) artifact.

none.basis: sugars, fats/oils, water, salt, most acids/flavorings carry no phe

cereal protein.basis: FDC-derived: rice white 53.4, potato flour 45.8; corroborated by typical cereal AA composition

vegetable protein.basis: FDC-derived mean of seed vegetables (tomato 30.7, broccoli 41.5, cucumber 52.5, carrot 65.6)

fruit protein.basis: FDC-derived mean of seed fruits (apple 25.9, strawberry 28.4, banana 45.0)

refined starch.basis: cornstarch/tapioca: protein negligible; contribution ~1 mg/serving, treated as 0

dairy protein.basis: typical AA composition (milk/casein/whey); refine via food list — no seed data yet

legume protein.basis: typical AA composition (soy/pea/bean); refine via food list — no seed data yet

egg protein.basis: typical AA composition (whole egg); refine via food list — no seed data yet

nut/seed protein.basis: typical AA composition; refine via food list — no seed data yet

gelatin/collagen.basis: typical AA composition; gelatin is low in aromatic amino acids — refine via food list

unknown protein.basis: conservative central default when class cannot be determined (~mid of observed range)
